findings.description: The UserForm constructor `public UserForm(Map<String, Object> params)` is omitted from the knowledge file's Java code example. The source shows how the Form initializes each property (`user`, `phoneNo1`, `phoneNo2`, `phoneNo3`) by casting values from the params map. This is a core implementation detail of the Form class — it shows how screen input values flow into the Form properties before validation.

The HTML form code example is omitted. The source contains a complete `<form>` snippet demonstrating the input field naming convention (`userForm.phoneNo1`, `userForm.phoneNo2`, `userForm.phoneNo3`) and the hidden field (`userForm.user.id`). This example establishes how the form field names correspond to the Form properties and is part of the implementation pattern.

findings.description: `validateAndConvertRequest` is missing from the hints. It is the key API method called in the Action code example and is an important searchable term for users implementing this pattern.
